stemHtml: What are the differences between steel and carbon brakes?

options.a: Only carbon brakes convert kinetic energy into heat energy through friction.
options.b: Only steel brakes convert kinetic energy into heat energy through friction.
options.c: Both carbon and steel brakes convert kinetic energy through friction into heat energy.
options.d: Both carbon and steel brakes convert kinetic energy through friction, however, steel is more efficient at transferring to heat energy.
